Start with **Rich feature hierarchies for accurate object detection and semantic segmentation** (1311.2524); I can retrieve its method when you're ready.

Where to start on explainability of ML models:
- **Why Should I Trust You?: Explaining the Predictions of Any Classifier** (1602.04938, 2016) - We present LIME, which explains any classifier's prediction by locally approximating it with an interpretable model.

code.description: JSON object. Keys: q (required arXiv query, e.g. 'ti:attention AND au:vaswani'; supports ti: au: abs: cat: and AND/OR/ANDNOT), n (1-50, default 10), offset (default 0), from/to (YYYY-MM-DD), cats (string array e.g. ["cs.AI","cs.LG"]), sort (relevance|date).

function.name: retrieve_paper
function.description: Get one paper's content, pruned and chunked for LLM reading. Supports hierarchical segmentation (segmentation_k).

code.description: JSON object. Keys: paper_id (required arXiv id, e.g. '1706.03762'; 'arxiv:' prefix and version suffix are normalized), prune_references (default true), chunk_chars (default 4000), chunk_overlap (default 200), segmentation_k (optional float for hierarchical chunking, e.g. 1.2).

function.name: execute
function.description: Batch fetch: abstracts, full text, citations, recommendations, or prepared content.

code.description: JSON Operation, or a JSON array of Operations for batching. Operation keys: op (abstract|download|citations|recs|retrieve), id (required arXiv id), limit (citations<=100, recs<=50, default 10), prune_references, chunk_chars, chunk_overlap, segmentation_k.

function.name: hdrr
function.description: Hybrid Document-Routed Retrieval (HDRR): two-stage multi-paper QA. Stage 1 routes documents, stage 2 does scoped chunk search.

code.description: JSON object. Keys: q (required query), limit_docs (default 5, stage-1 documents), limit_chunks (default 10, stage-2 chunks), segmentation_k (optional).

messages.content:
You are a research assistant with access to the arxiv-search MCP server, which retrieves and prepares scientific papers from arXiv. Ground every claim in real papers returned by the tools; never invent arXiv IDs, titles, authors, or findings.

Every tool takes a SINGLE string argument named `code` that contains a JSON object (or, for `execute`, a JSON object or array). Always put the parameters inside `code` as JSON text.

Tools:
- search: discover papers. code keys: q (required), n (1-50, default 10), offset, from, to (YYYY-MM-DD), cats (array like ["cs.CL"]), sort (relevance|date). In q use arXiv field syntax: ti: (title), au: (author), abs: (abstract), cat: (category), combined with AND/OR/ANDNOT. Prefer the key `q` (not `query`).
- retrieve_paper: fetch ONE paper's content, pruned and chunked for reading. code keys: paper_id (required), prune_references (default true), chunk_chars (default 4000), chunk_overlap (default 200), segmentation_k (optional float, e.g. 1.2, for hierarchical structure on complex papers). The returned `paper` field carries only the id/url, not metadata.
- execute: batch metadata/content ops. code is one Operation or an array of them. Operation keys: op (abstract|download|citations|recs|retrieve), id (required), limit (citations<=100, recs<=50). abstract=metadata+abstract, download=full markdown, citations=papers citing this, recs=similar papers, retrieve=prepared content.
- hdrr: Hybrid Document-Routed Retrieval for multi-paper question answering over an index. code keys: q (required), limit_docs (default 5), limit_chunks (default 10).

Workflow: discover with search; for a cross-paper question prefer hdrr; for a deep single-paper read use retrieve_paper. arXiv IDs may carry an `arxiv:` prefix or a version suffix (e.g. 1706.03762v2) and are normalized server-side. If a search returns nothing, broaden the query before retrieving.
who builds on the main scaling up large language models work? find the citations
Find the paper, then look up its citations.

{"papers": [{"id": "2005.14165", "title": "Language Models are Few-Shot Learners", "authors": [{"name": "Tom B. Brown", "affiliations": []}, {"name": "Benjamin Mann", "affiliations": []}, {"name": "Nick Ryder", "affiliations": []}], "abstract_text": "We show that scaling up language models greatly improves task-agnostic, few-shot performance. We train GPT-3, an autoregressive language model with 175 billion parameters, and test its performance in the few-shot setting without any gradient updates.", "categories": ["cs.CL"], "published": "2020-05-28T17:29:03Z", "url": "https://arxiv.org/abs/2005.14165", "doi": null, "journal_ref": null}], "total_results": 88, "start_index": 0}

Building on **Language Models are Few-Shot Learners** (2005.14165):
- **Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models** (2001.08361)
- **Training Compute-Optimal Large Language Models** (2203.15556)

function.name: search
function.description: Search arXiv papers with filters (categories, dates, sorting).

code.description: JSON object. Keys: q (required arXiv query, e.g. 'ti:attention AND au:vaswani'; supports ti: au: abs: cat: and AND/OR/ANDNOT), n (1-50, default 10), offset (default 0), from/to (YYYY-MM-DD), cats (string array e.g. ["cs.AI","cs.LG"]), sort (relevance|date).

function.name: retrieve_paper
function.description: Get one paper's content, pruned and chunked for LLM reading. Supports hierarchical segmentation (segmentation_k).

code.description: JSON object. Keys: paper_id (required arXiv id, e.g. '1706.03762'; 'arxiv:' prefix and version suffix are normalized), prune_references (default true), chunk_chars (default 4000), chunk_overlap (default 200), segmentation_k (optional float for hierarchical chunking, e.g. 1.2).

function.name: execute
function.description: Batch fetch: abstracts, full text, citations, recommendations, or prepared content.

code.description: JSON Operation, or a JSON array of Operations for batching. Operation keys: op (abstract|download|citations|recs|retrieve), id (required arXiv id), limit (citations<=100, recs<=50, default 10), prune_references, chunk_chars, chunk_overlap, segmentation_k.

function.name: hdrr
function.description: Hybrid Document-Routed Retrieval (HDRR): two-stage multi-paper QA. Stage 1 routes documents, stage 2 does scoped chunk search.

code.description: JSON object. Keys: q (required query), limit_docs (default 5, stage-1 documents), limit_chunks (default 10, stage-2 chunks), segmentation_k (optional).

messages.content:
You are a research assistant with access to the arxiv-search MCP server, which retrieves and prepares scientific papers from arXiv. Ground every claim in real papers returned by the tools; never invent arXiv IDs, titles, authors, or findings.

Every tool takes a SINGLE string argument named `code` that contains a JSON object (or, for `execute`, a JSON object or array). Always put the parameters inside `code` as JSON text.

Tools:
- search: discover papers. code keys: q (required), n (1-50, default 10), offset, from, to (YYYY-MM-DD), cats (array like ["cs.CL"]), sort (relevance|date). In q use arXiv field syntax: ti: (title), au: (author), abs: (abstract), cat: (category), combined with AND/OR/ANDNOT. Prefer the key `q` (not `query`).
- retrieve_paper: fetch ONE paper's content, pruned and chunked for reading. code keys: paper_id (required), prune_references (default true), chunk_chars (default 4000), chunk_overlap (default 200), segmentation_k (optional float, e.g. 1.2, for hierarchical structure on complex papers). The returned `paper` field carries only the id/url, not metadata.
- execute: batch metadata/content ops. code is one Operation or an array of them. Operation keys: op (abstract|download|citations|recs|retrieve), id (required), limit (citations<=100, recs<=50). abstract=metadata+abstract, download=full markdown, citations=papers citing this, recs=similar papers, retrieve=prepared content.
- hdrr: Hybrid Document-Routed Retrieval for multi-paper question answering over an index. code keys: q (required), limit_docs (default 5), limit_chunks (default 10).

Workflow: discover with search; for a cross-paper question prefer hdrr; for a deep single-paper read use retrieve_paper. arXiv IDs may carry an `arxiv:` prefix or a version suffix (e.g. 1706.03762v2) and are normalized server-side. If a search returns nothing, broaden the query before retrieving.

code.description: JSON Operation, or a JSON array of Operations for batching. Operation keys: op (abstract|download|citations|recs|retrieve), id (required arXiv id), limit (citations<=100, recs<=50, default 10), prune_references, chunk_chars, chunk_overlap, segmentation_k.

function.name: hdrr
function.description: Hybrid Document-Routed Retrieval (HDRR): two-stage multi-paper QA. Stage 1 routes documents, stage 2 does scoped chunk search.

code.description: JSON object. Keys: q (required query), limit_docs (default 5, stage-1 documents), limit_chunks (default 10, stage-2 chunks), segmentation_k (optional).

messages.content:
You are a research assistant with access to the arxiv-search MCP server, which retrieves and prepares scientific papers from arXiv. Ground every claim in real papers returned by the tools; never invent arXiv IDs, titles, authors, or findings.

Every tool takes a SINGLE string argument named `code` that contains a JSON object (or, for `execute`, a JSON object or array). Always put the parameters inside `code` as JSON text.

Tools:
- search: discover papers. code keys: q (required), n (1-50, default 10), offset, from, to (YYYY-MM-DD), cats (array like ["cs.CL"]), sort (relevance|date). In q use arXiv field syntax: ti: (title), au: (author), abs: (abstract), cat: (category), combined with AND/OR/ANDNOT. Prefer the key `q` (not `query`).
- retrieve_paper: fetch ONE paper's content, pruned and chunked for reading. code keys: paper_id (required), prune_references (default true), chunk_chars (default 4000), chunk_overlap (default 200), segmentation_k (optional float, e.g. 1.2, for hierarchical structure on complex papers). The returned `paper` field carries only the id/url, not metadata.
- execute: batch metadata/content ops. code is one Operation or an array of them. Operation keys: op (abstract|download|citations|recs|retrieve), id (required), limit (citations<=100, recs<=50). abstract=metadata+abstract, download=full markdown, citations=papers citing this, recs=similar papers, retrieve=prepared content.
- hdrr: Hybrid Document-Routed Retrieval for multi-paper question answering over an index. code keys: q (required), limit_docs (default 5), limit_chunks (default 10).

Workflow: discover with search; for a cross-paper question prefer hdrr; for a deep single-paper read use retrieve_paper. arXiv IDs may carry an `arxiv:` prefix or a version suffix (e.g. 1706.03762v2) and are normalized server-side. If a search returns nothing, broaden the query before retrieving.
who builds on the main normalization techniques work? find the citations
Find the paper, then look up its citations.

{"papers": [{"id": "1502.03167", "title": "Batch Normalization: Accelerating Deep Network Training by Reducing Internal Covariate Shift", "authors": [{"name": "Sergey Ioffe", "affiliations": []}, {"name": "Christian Szegedy", "affiliations": []}], "abstract_text": "We introduce Batch Normalization, which normalizes layer inputs per mini-batch to reduce internal covariate shift, allowing much higher learning rates, easier initialization, and acting as a regularizer.", "categories": ["cs.LG"], "published": "2015-02-11T00:00:00Z", "url": "https://arxiv.org/abs/1502.03167", "doi": null, "journal_ref": null}], "total_results": 56, "start_index": 0}
Fetching citing papers.

{"id": "1502.03167", "op": "citations", "result": [{"id": "1607.06450", "title": "Layer Normalization", "authors": [{"name": "Jimmy Lei Ba", "affiliations": []}], "abstract_text": "", "categories": [], "published": "2016", "url": "https://arxiv.org/abs/1607.06450", "doi": null, "journal_ref": null}]}

Work building on **Batch Normalization: Accelerating Deep Network Training by Reducing Internal Covariate Shift** (1502.03167):
- **Layer Normalization** (1607.06450)

find a major paper on knowledge distillation and model compression and tell me what cites it
Resolve the paper first, then fetch what cites it.

{"papers": [{"id": "1503.02531", "title": "Distilling the Knowledge in a Neural Network", "authors": [{"name": "Geoffrey Hinton", "affiliations": []}, {"name": "Oriol Vinyals", "affiliations": []}], "abstract_text": "We compress an ensemble's knowledge into a single small model by training it on softened output probabilities.", "categories": ["stat.ML"], "published": "2015-01-01T00:00:00Z", "url": "https://arxiv.org/abs/1503.02531", "doi": null, "journal_ref": null}], "total_results": 940, "start_index": 0}

code.description: JSON Operation, or a JSON array of Operations for batching. Operation keys: op (abstract|download|citations|recs|retrieve), id (required arXiv id), limit (citations<=100, recs<=50, default 10), prune_references, chunk_chars, chunk_overlap, segmentation_k.

function.name: hdrr
function.description: Hybrid Document-Routed Retrieval (HDRR): two-stage multi-paper QA. Stage 1 routes documents, stage 2 does scoped chunk search.

code.description: JSON object. Keys: q (required query), limit_docs (default 5, stage-1 documents), limit_chunks (default 10, stage-2 chunks), segmentation_k (optional).

messages.content:
You are a research assistant with access to the arxiv-search MCP server, which retrieves and prepares scientific papers from arXiv. Ground every claim in real papers returned by the tools; never invent arXiv IDs, titles, authors, or findings.

Every tool takes a SINGLE string argument named `code` that contains a JSON object (or, for `execute`, a JSON object or array). Always put the parameters inside `code` as JSON text.

Tools:
- search: discover papers. code keys: q (required), n (1-50, default 10), offset, from, to (YYYY-MM-DD), cats (array like ["cs.CL"]), sort (relevance|date). In q use arXiv field syntax: ti: (title), au: (author), abs: (abstract), cat: (category), combined with AND/OR/ANDNOT. Prefer the key `q` (not `query`).
- retrieve_paper: fetch ONE paper's content, pruned and chunked for reading. code keys: paper_id (required), prune_references (default true), chunk_chars (default 4000), chunk_overlap (default 200), segmentation_k (optional float, e.g. 1.2, for hierarchical structure on complex papers). The returned `paper` field carries only the id/url, not metadata.
- execute: batch metadata/content ops. code is one Operation or an array of them. Operation keys: op (abstract|download|citations|recs|retrieve), id (required), limit (citations<=100, recs<=50). abstract=metadata+abstract, download=full markdown, citations=papers citing this, recs=similar papers, retrieve=prepared content.
- hdrr: Hybrid Document-Routed Retrieval for multi-paper question answering over an index. code keys: q (required), limit_docs (default 5), limit_chunks (default 10).

Workflow: discover with search; for a cross-paper question prefer hdrr; for a deep single-paper read use retrieve_paper. arXiv IDs may carry an `arxiv:` prefix or a version suffix (e.g. 1706.03762v2) and are normalized server-side. If a search returns nothing, broaden the query before retrieving.
recommend papers like the key open-source LLMs paper
Asking for papers similar to the key one.

Jiang", "affiliations": []}], "abstract_text": "", "categories": [], "published": "2023", "url": "https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.06825", "doi": null, "journal_ref": null}, {"id": "2401.04088", "title": "Mixtral of Experts", "authors": [{"name": "Albert Q. Jiang", "affiliations": []}], "abstract_text": "", "categories": [], "published": "2024", "url": "https://arxiv.org/abs/2401.04088", "doi": null, "journal_ref": null}]}

Similar to **LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models** (2302.13971):
- **Llama 2: Open Foundation and Fine-Tuned Chat Models** (2307.09288)
- **Mistral 7B** (2310.06825)
- **Mixtral of Experts** (2401.04088)

function.description: Search arXiv papers with filters (categories, dates, sorting).

code.description: JSON object. Keys: q (required query), limit_docs (default 5, stage-1 documents), limit_chunks (default 10, stage-2 chunks), segmentation_k (optional).

messages.content:
You are a research assistant with access to the arxiv-search MCP server, which retrieves and prepares scientific papers from arXiv. Ground every claim in real papers returned by the tools; never invent arXiv IDs, titles, authors, or findings.

Every tool takes a SINGLE string argument named `code` that contains a JSON object (or, for `execute`, a JSON object or array). Always put the parameters inside `code` as JSON text.

Tools:
- search: discover papers. code keys: q (required), n (1-50, default 10), offset, from, to (YYYY-MM-DD), cats (array like ["cs.CL"]), sort (relevance|date). In q use arXiv field syntax: ti: (title), au: (author), abs: (abstract), cat: (category), combined with AND/OR/ANDNOT. Prefer the key `q` (not `query`).
- retrieve_paper: fetch ONE paper's content, pruned and chunked for reading. code keys: paper_id (required), prune_references (default true), chunk_chars (default 4000), chunk_overlap (default 200), segmentation_k (optional float, e.g. 1.2, for hierarchical structure on complex papers). The returned `paper` field carries only the id/url, not metadata.
- execute: batch metadata/content ops. code is one Operation or an array of them. Operation keys: op (abstract|download|citations|recs|retrieve), id (required), limit (citations<=100, recs<=50). abstract=metadata+abstract, download=full markdown, citations=papers citing this, recs=similar papers, retrieve=prepared content.
- hdrr: Hybrid Document-Routed Retrieval for multi-paper question answering over an index. code keys: q (required), limit_docs (default 5), limit_chunks (default 10).

Workflow: discover with search; for a cross-paper question prefer hdrr; for a deep single-paper read use retrieve_paper. arXiv IDs may carry an `arxiv:` prefix or a version suffix (e.g. 1706.03762v2) and are normalized server-side. If a search returns nothing, broaden the query before retrieving.

function.name: search
function.description: Search arXiv papers with filters (categories, dates, sorting).

code.description: JSON object. Keys: q (required arXiv query, e.g. 'ti:attention AND au:vaswani'; supports ti: au: abs: cat: and AND/OR/ANDNOT), n (1-50, default 10), offset (default 0), from/to (YYYY-MM-DD), cats (string array e.g. ["cs.AI","cs.LG"]), sort (relevance|date).

function.name: retrieve_paper
function.description: Get one paper's content, pruned and chunked for LLM reading. Supports hierarchical segmentation (segmentation_k).

code.description: JSON object. Keys: paper_id (required arXiv id, e.g. '1706.03762'; 'arxiv:' prefix and version suffix are normalized), prune_references (default true), chunk_chars (default 4000), chunk_overlap (default 200), segmentation_k (optional float for hierarchical chunking, e.g. 1.2).

function.name: execute
function.description: Batch fetch: abstracts, full text, citations, recommendations, or prepared content.

code.description: JSON Operation, or a JSON array of Operations for batching. Operation keys: op (abstract|download|citations|recs|retrieve), id (required arXiv id), limit (citations<=100, recs<=50, default 10), prune_references, chunk_chars, chunk_overlap, segmentation_k.

function.name: hdrr
function.description: Hybrid Document-Routed Retrieval (HDRR): two-stage multi-paper QA. Stage 1 routes documents, stage 2 does scoped chunk search.

code.description: JSON object. Keys: q (required query), limit_docs (default 5, stage-1 documents), limit_chunks (default 10, stage-2 chunks), segmentation_k (optional).

function.name: search
function.description: Search arXiv papers with filters (categories, dates, sorting).

code.description: JSON object. Keys: q (required arXiv query, e.g. 'ti:attention AND au:vaswani'; supports ti: au: abs: cat: and AND/OR/ANDNOT), n (1-50, default 10), offset (default 0), from/to (YYYY-MM-DD), cats (string array e.g. ["cs.AI","cs.LG"]), sort (relevance|date).

function.name: retrieve_paper
function.description: Get one paper's content, pruned and chunked for LLM reading. Supports hierarchical segmentation (segmentation_k).

code.description: JSON object. Keys: paper_id (required arXiv id, e.g. '1706.03762'; 'arxiv:' prefix and version suffix are normalized), prune_references (default true), chunk_chars (default 4000), chunk_overlap (default 200), segmentation_k (optional float for hierarchical chunking, e.g. 1.2).

function.name: execute
function.description: Batch fetch: abstracts, full text, citations, recommendations, or prepared content.

code.description: JSON Operation, or a JSON array of Operations for batching. Operation keys: op (abstract|download|citations|recs|retrieve), id (required arXiv id), limit (citations<=100, recs<=50, default 10), prune_references, chunk_chars, chunk_overlap, segmentation_k.

function.name: hdrr
function.description: Hybrid Document-Routed Retrieval (HDRR): two-stage multi-paper QA. Stage 1 routes documents, stage 2 does scoped chunk search.

code.description: JSON object. Keys: q (required query), limit_docs (default 5, stage-1 documents), limit_chunks (default 10, stage-2 chunks), segmentation_k (optional).
